eprint.title: BSPL: Balanced Static Power Logic
eprint.abstract: The down-scaling of circuit technology has led to stronger leakage currents in CMOS standard cells. This source of power consumption is data dependent and can be utilized to extract secrets from cryptographic devices. We propose Balanced Static Power Logic (BSPL), the first leakage-balancing approach that achieves optimal data-independence with respect to drain-source leakage. We re-design fundamental standard cells in such a way that their leakage current is essentially constant, irrespective of inputs and outputs, barring process variations. Even in presence of considerable intra-die variations, modeled by Monte Carlo simulations, BSPL gates still maintain a significantly reduced mutual information between the circuit’s input and conducted leakage current.
